title.zh: 大腦煞車失靈？科學家發現強迫症「當機時刻」的關鍵腦波
title.en: The Brain's Broken Brake: Scientists Pinpoint Gamma Wave Signature of OCD Symptom Flares

summary.zh:
當強迫症患者陷入反覆洗手或檢查門鎖的『當機循環』時，他們的大腦究竟發生了什麼？《Cell》最新研究透過植入式電極發現，大腦『煞車系統』的異常放電可能是關鍵。

研究團隊在患者進行症狀誘發任務時，直接記錄了眶額葉皮質(orbitofrontal cortex, OFC)的神經活動。結果顯示，當症狀加劇時，OFC前內側區的低伽馬波(30-50Hz)會異常活躍，就像卡住的汽車油門不斷空轉。更驚人的是，當用電刺激抑制基底核的異常活動後，不僅患者症狀減輕，OFC的伽馬波也隨之下降。

這項發現為深層腦刺激(DBS)療法提供了精準的生物標記，未來或能根據即時腦波變化調整治療強度。主導研究的Nho博士比喻：『我們終於找到強迫症這台失靈機器的故障代碼，接下來就能編寫修復程式。』
summary.en:
In a groundbreaking study published in Cell, neuroscientists have identified a precise neural signature of OCD symptom exacerbation. By recording intracranial brain activity in patients undergoing symptom provocation, the team discovered that spikes in low-gamma (30-50Hz) oscillations in the anteromedial orbitofrontal cortex (OFC) consistently marked moments of intense compulsive urges.

The research goes beyond correlation to demonstrate causation: When clinicians applied therapeutic electrical stimulation to the ventral basal ganglia—a known target for deep brain stimulation (DBS) in OCD—both symptom relief and reduced gamma power in the OFC occurred simultaneously. This dual observation suggests the OFC's gamma activity isn't merely a bystander but plays an active role in symptom generation.

These findings revolutionize our understanding of OCD's neural mechanics, offering clinicians what senior author Dr. Halpern calls 'a real-time biomarker for tuning neuromodulation therapies.' The study paves the way for closed-loop DBS systems that could automatically adjust stimulation based on moment-to-moment brain activity, potentially transforming treatment for the 2-3% of people worldwide living with debilitating OCD.

keyArticle.title: Human orbitofrontal neural activity is linked to obsessive-compulsive behavioral dynamics
